payload.answer: Knowledge graphs are graph-based data structures that represent entities as nodes and relationships as edges, enabling semantic understanding and reasoning. They power search, recommendations, and linked data systems. What specific aspect interests you—construction methods, use cases, or technologies?
payload.decision: Create foundational memory cards about knowledge graphs with high-level concepts and defer specialized details pending user clarification

entity_resolution.reason: User inquiry is open-ended; no specific knowledge graph entity referenced; foundational cards created to support future disambiguation

memory_cards.statement: A knowledge graph is a structured representation of information that uses nodes (entities) and edges (relationships) to model real-world concepts and their interconnections
Knowledge graphs are used in search engines, recommendation systems, semantic web applications, and enterprise data management

repair_options: Clarify whether user wants technical implementation details, business applications, or theoretical foundations
Ask about specific knowledge graph systems (Google Knowledge Graph, DBpedia, Wikidata, etc.)
Determine if user needs to build, query, or understand existing knowledge graphs
